title: Heatwave tightens grip on Odisha, Jharsuguda sizzles at 39.6 deg C - Odisha TV
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat") Odisha
full_text:
The rise in daytime temperatures with the onset of March has intensified heat conditions across Odisha, with Jharsuguda recording the highest temperature of 39.6 degrees Celsius in the past 24 hours.
According to the Regional Meteorological Centre in Bhubaneswar, while the prevailing heat is likely to persist, no significant change in temperature is expected across the state over the next five days.
Temperature Trend Across The State
The increase in daytime temperature has led to warmer conditions in several parts of the state since the beginning of March. Jharsuguda recorded the highest temperature of 39.6 degrees Celsius during the last 24 hours, indicating the gradual rise in heat levels.
Meteorological officials have indicated that the overall temperature pattern is expected to remain largely stable in the coming days. Despite the absence of any major fluctuations in temperature, the prevailing warm conditions are likely to continue across Odisha.
Forecast For The Coming Days
The Regional Meteorological Centre has stated that there will be no significant change in temperature across the state during the next five days. As a result, the existing heat conditions are expected to persist during this period.
However, the weather pattern in some parts of the state may witness a change later in the week.
Possibility Of Rain In North Odisha
According to the forecast, weather conditions in North Odisha are likely to change by March 8. There is a possibility of rainfall accompanied by lightning and thunder in the region.
The development may bring temporary relief in areas where temperatures have been rising steadily since the beginning of the month.
